fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` with `remaining=0` instead of `False, 0`.

constraints.intent: When recent count equals limit, the strict > check fails to trigger the denial branch, exposing the off-by-one bug.
After the else branch (request allowed), recent count must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been denied.
If recent count is at or above limit, the strict > guard must also be true; otherwise the boundary case is missed.
The function should never return (True, 0): allowed=True with remaining=0 is an inconsistent state indicating the boundary check is wrong.
